title: Rust Stream
description: Rust Stream documents async stream material for Rust in the language domain. It is classified as a concept page for the programming-language class, so readers can understand Stream as the async counterpart to Iterator, consume streams with StreamExt, create streams with async-stream, bridge Tokio channels with tokio-stream, and return streams from Axum SSE handlers. Important search terms include rust, stream, async-stream, futures, tokio-stream, axum, sse, and language, helping both humans and generated indexes connect this page with adjacent async Rust material. The route is language / rust / stream, and the frontmatter keeps the canonical subject, ontology role, and navigation context aligned with repository validation.
